signals.headline: US and Iran Agree to 2-Week Ceasefire as Trump Deescalates Threats of Strikes on Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**[1][2]
signals.raw:
US and Iran Agree to 2-Week Ceasefire as Trump Deescalates Threats of Strikes on Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**[1][2]

A 2-week ceasefire was announced on April 7, 2026, with Trump pulling back from strikes just before his deadline for Iran, shifting to diplomacy amid ongoing talks.[1][2] Fresh negotiations are eyed before it expires next week after weekend talks in Islamabad stalled, per sources
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?

signals.headline: South Korea's Annual Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's Annual Inflation Rises to 2.2% in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in the prior two months, below market forecasts of 2.4%, as confirmed by Statistics Korea data released recently.[1]  
This uptick from the Bank of Korea's 2% target, amid contained price pressures before Middle East energy volatility,
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: US Begins Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports in Strait of Hormuz | YES | HIGH confidence**[3][4]
signals.raw:
US Begins Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports in Strait of Hormuz | YES | HIGH confidence**[3][4]

Trump declared the blockade effective, targeting Iranian oil tankers while monitoring others; Iran vows retaliation, and fast-attack ships risk elimination.[3][4] Ceasefire holds fragilely with no US Navy attacks but drone strikes on Gulf allies like Bahrain reported.[4]
